title: Sega Saturn Platform Stack
body:
# Sega Saturn Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `console_saturn` · **Era:** 1994–1998 · **Vendor:** Sega · **Count:** 7

Dual Hitachi **SH-2** CPUs with SCU system control, VDP1/VDP2 graphics, and SMPC peripheral hub — launched **November 1994** in Japan.

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: console_saturn` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **Sega 315-5570 SCU** (Sega, 32-bit) — System Control Unit — DSP DMA between SH-2 and VDPs
- **Sega VDP1** (Sega, 32-bit) — Sprite/polygon renderer — framebuffer VRAM
- **Sega VDP2** (Sega, 32-bit) — Background scroll planes — tile/sprite priority
- **Sega SMPC** (Sega, 32-bit) — System Manager & Peripheral Control — reset/RTC/pad
- **Saturn Dual SH-2 Glue** (Sega, 32-bit) — Master/slave SH-2 bus matrix + shared RAM arbiter
- **Saturn SCSP Audio** (Sega, 32-bit) — Sound CPU + 32 PCM voices — Saturn audio path

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **7** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: console_saturn · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
